name: The Clockmaker's Mansion
description: You awaken in the foyer of an old mansion. The front door is bolted with seven locks. A note on the floor reads: 'Welcome, guests. I am the Clockmaker, and this is my finest puzzle. Find the seven keys hidden in my home, or remain here forever. The clock is ticking.' Somewhere deep in the house, a grandfather clock begins to chime.

foyer.name: The Grand Foyer
foyer.description: A sweeping entrance hall with a checkered marble floor and a crystal chandelier hanging dark overhead. The front door behind you is massive oak, reinforced with iron bands and fitted with seven keyholes — all locked. A grand staircase curves upward but a velvet rope blocks it, clipped to a brass post with a small combination lock. Hallways lead east to a parlor and west to a dining room. A faded portrait of a stern old man — the Clockmaker — watches from above the door.

parlor.name: The Parlor
parlor.description: A sitting room frozen in time. Velvet armchairs circle a cold fireplace. A grandfather clock in the corner ticks steadily, its pendulum swinging behind glass. Bookshelves line the north wall, and one book — bound in red leather — sits slightly pulled out. A door to the south is locked with an ornate brass mechanism. The foyer is west.

dining-room.name: The Dining Room
dining-room.description: A long mahogany table set for a dinner that was never served. Tarnished candelabras, fine china coated in dust, crystal goblets still half-full of something dark and dried. A serving hatch in the north wall leads to the kitchen, but it's jammed shut by something heavy on the other side. The foyer is east. A narrow door to the south is marked with a painted musical note.
hazards: The candelabra flames flicker unnervingly — there's a draft from somewhere you can't see.

music-room.name: The Music Room
music-room.description: A small, acoustically perfect chamber. A grand piano dominates the center, its lid propped open to reveal dusty strings. Sheet music sits on the stand — a composition titled 'The Clockmaker's Lullaby.' The walls are lined with instruments: violins, a harp, a cello. One violin case is open but empty, with a velvet impression of something other than a violin. The dining room is north.

kitchen.name: The Kitchen
kitchen.description: A vast industrial kitchen with copper pots hanging from ceiling racks. An ancient wood-burning stove still radiates faint warmth. The pantry door to the west is padlocked. On the counter sits a mortar and pestle with traces of green powder. The serving hatch south leads back to the dining room. A narrow servants' staircase climbs north into darkness.
hazards: The gas line hisses faintly — don't strike any sparks near the stove.

study.name: The Clockmaker's Study
study.description: The Clockmaker's private sanctum. A massive desk covered in technical drawings of impossibly complex clockwork mechanisms. Gears and springs are scattered everywhere. A half-assembled clock sits on the desk, missing its mainspring. A wall safe behind a painting is visible but locked with a four-digit combination. The hallway is south. A locked door to the north has a keyhole shaped like a musical note.

upper-hallway.name: The Upper Hallway
upper-hallway.description: A shadowy corridor at the top of the grand staircase. Portrait frames line the walls — but every painting has been removed, leaving only dust outlines and hanging wires. The carpet is worn in a path between doors. Moonlight streams through a cracked window at the end of the hall. The study is north. A guest bedroom lies to the east. The stairs descend south to the foyer.

guest-bedroom.name: The Guest Bedroom
guest-bedroom.description: A four-poster bed with moth-eaten curtains. The dresser mirror is cracked in a spiderweb pattern — and in the reflection, you notice the wallpaper behind the bed has a slightly different pattern. A wardrobe stands against the west wall, its door ajar. On the nightstand, a music box sits silent. The upper hallway is west.

secret-workshop.name: The Secret Workshop
secret-workshop.description: Behind the wallpaper, a hidden room. The Clockmaker's true workshop — where he built his puzzles. Workbenches overflow with tiny gears, springs, and half-finished automatons. A mechanical bird sits in a cage, frozen mid-song. In the center, a clockwork door with seven keyhole slots stands between you and a passage east. Each slot is a different shape. The guest bedroom is west.

observatory.name: The Observatory
observatory.description: The pinnacle of the mansion — a domed room with a brass telescope pointed at the night sky. The ceiling is painted with constellations, and small brass stars are embedded in the plaster. A complex orrery — a mechanical model of the solar system — sits on a central pedestal, its planets frozen in position. A hatch in the floor leads down to the study. Against the wall sits a cabinet with a glass door, and inside it gleams the final piece of the Clockmaker's puzzle — the master key.

welcome-note.name: Welcome Note
welcome-note.description: A handwritten note on aged parchment: 'Seven locks, seven keys. Each room holds a piece. Work together, think together, escape together. — The Clockmaker.' On the back is scrawled: '4-7-1-9'.
welcome-note.pickupText: You pick up the welcome note. The handwriting is meticulous, almost mechanical in its precision. The number on the back might be important.

red-book.name: Red Book
red-book.description: A leather-bound volume titled 'The Art of Hidden Passages.' A chapter is bookmarked: 'Behind the wallpaper, the truth awaits. Look for the pattern that doesn't match.'
red-book.pickupText: You pull the red book from the shelf. Something clicks behind the bookcase, but nothing else happens... yet.

silver-fork.name: Silver Fork
silver-fork.description: An ornate silver fork from the place setting. Unusually heavy. The handle is engraved with tiny musical notes — it's actually a tuning fork disguised as cutlery.
silver-fork.pickupText: You take the silver fork. When you lift it, it hums with a clear, ringing tone. This is no ordinary utensil.

sheet-music.name: Sheet Music
sheet-music.description: The Clockmaker's Lullaby — a short composition. At the bottom, handwritten: 'Play this to open what sound has sealed.' The melody is hauntingly simple.
sheet-music.pickupText: You take the sheet music from the piano stand. The notes seem to shimmer as you read them.

iron-key.name: Iron Key
iron-key.description: A heavy iron key with teeth cut in an unusual zigzag pattern. A tag reads 'PANTRY — DO NOT LOSE'.
iron-key.pickupText: You take the iron key from the counter. It's cold and heavy in your hand.

gear-piece.name: Gear Piece
gear-piece.description: A precision-machined brass gear with the Clockmaker's mark stamped on one side. It looks like it belongs in a larger mechanism — perhaps the clock in the parlor.
gear-piece.pickupText: You pick up the gear piece. It fits perfectly in your palm, warm from the desk lamp above it.

music-box.name: Music Box
music-box.description: A delicate clockwork music box with a tiny ballerina inside. It's wound but won't play — a small gear is missing from the mechanism. The tune it's meant to play is inscribed on the base: 'The Clockmaker's Lullaby.'
music-box.pickupText: You take the music box from the nightstand. The ballerina trembles slightly, as if eager to dance.

clockwork-key.name: Clockwork Key
clockwork-key.description: An intricate key made entirely of interlocking gears and springs. It transforms as you hold it, teeth shifting into new configurations. This is the Clockmaker's masterwork.
clockwork-key.pickupText: You take the clockwork key from the workbench. Its gears shift in your hand, clicking into a new shape. Remarkable.

master-key.name: Master Key
master-key.description: A golden key with seven notches — one for each lock on the front door. The bow is shaped like a clock face frozen at midnight. This is it. The way out.
master-key.pickupText: You take the master key from the cabinet. It's heavier than it looks, as if it carries the weight of every puzzle you've solved to reach it. Freedom is close.

pantry-note.name: Pantry Note
pantry-note.description: A slip of paper pinned inside the pantry. It reads: 'The velvet rope remembers: three-two-five-eight. Or was it the safe? I forget which lock gets which code. — C.M.' A clue, perhaps, for somewhere else.
pantry-note.pickupText: You grab the slip of paper. Two codes, two locks — you'll need to figure out which goes where.

open-music-room.description: The narrow door to the south is sealed. The painted musical note on it suggests sound is the key. The silver fork from the table hums when lifted — could it resonate with the door?
open-music-room.solvedText: You strike the silver fork against the door frame. It rings with a pure, crystalline tone that resonates through the wood. The painted musical note glows briefly, and the door swings open to the south, revealing the music room!

open-kitchen.description: The serving hatch to the north is jammed. Something heavy blocks it from the kitchen side. The sheet music mentions that certain melodies can move what hands cannot.
open-kitchen.solvedText: You hold the sheet music up to the serving hatch and hum The Clockmaker's Lullaby. The notes seem to drift through the gap. On the other side, you hear clockwork mechanisms whir to life — whatever was blocking the hatch slides aside, and it swings open to the north, revealing the kitchen!

open-upstairs.description: A velvet rope blocks the grand staircase going north. It's clipped to a brass post with a small combination lock. The welcome note has a number on the back — 4-7-1-9.
open-upstairs.solvedText: You enter 4-7-1-9 on the combination lock. It clicks open and the velvet rope falls away. The grand staircase to the north is clear — the upper floor awaits!

open-secret-workshop.description: The wallpaper behind the bed has a different pattern — the red book mentioned hidden passages behind wallpaper. Perhaps pressing in the right spot will reveal what's hidden.
open-secret-workshop.solvedText: Following the instructions in the red book, you press the mismatched wallpaper section. It gives way with a click, and an entire panel swings inward, revealing a hidden workshop to the east! Dust motes swirl in the shaft of light that cuts into the secret room.

open-observatory.description: A locked door to the east has a keyhole shaped like a musical note. The music box from the guest bedroom plays The Clockmaker's Lullaby — and its gear housing matches this lock perfectly.
open-observatory.solvedText: You hold the music box up to the musical keyhole. Its mechanism interlocks with the lock — the ballerina begins to spin, playing The Clockmaker's Lullaby. The door clicks open to the north, revealing a spiral staircase up to an observatory!

fix-music-box.description: The music box on the nightstand is missing a small gear. The gear piece from the Clockmaker's study might fit its mechanism.
fix-music-box.solvedText: You carefully insert the gear piece into the music box mechanism. It clicks into place perfectly. The music box whirs to life, and the tiny ballerina begins to dance as The Clockmaker's Lullaby fills the room. The repaired music box is now in your hands — it might serve as a key elsewhere.

clear-gas-hazard.description: The gas line hisses dangerously near the stove. The iron key might fit the gas shutoff valve hidden behind the counter.
clear-gas-hazard.solvedText: You use the iron key to turn the gas shutoff valve behind the counter. The hissing stops immediately, and the oppressive tension in the room lifts. You can move freely now without fear of an explosion. You also notice a slip of paper that was hidden behind the valve.

action.hazard: The gas line hisses faintly — don't strike any sparks near the stove.
